Seemingly imprevious to traditional weaponry, Godzilla will destroy Manhattan unless scientist Dr. Nick Tatopolous and mysterious French insurance investigator Philippe Roache discover how to stop the monster s rampage. GODZILLA is directed by Roland Emerich and written by Dean Devlin, the team behind INDEPENDENCE DAY.

programme.title: The Contractor

But Dial learns that once his mission is accomplished, he will be next on the hit list, to remove any traces of American involvement. Aided by an 11-year-old neighbor girl, Dial must find a way to stay one step ahead of his hunter.

programme.title: The Game That Kills

programme.desc: Charley Stevens moves to the town where her recently deceased mother grew up; after arriving, she is plagued with strange nightmares that tease a horrific event, hinting at why her mother left town.

programme.title: Spiritwalker

programme.title: Storm Cell
programme.desc: Storm chaser April, whose parents died in a twister during her childhood, must warn her brother and his unsespecting town of a massive storm heading their way before he meets the same fate.

programme.title: Meet Me in New York
programme.desc: Events planner Kelly meets her match in web editor Joe when planning his niece's birthday party.

programme.title: At Home With Tori
programme.desc: Tori teams up with Josh Flagg to create a backyard summer camp-out with the Troop Beverly Hills theme.

programme.title: Best Friend From Heaven
programme.desc: When her dog is killed, Tara cancels her wedding. But her dog's ghost and her small town have a surprise.

programme.title: My Mother's Stalker
programme.desc: Old friends gets together for a high school reunion, only for a crime to set off a series of tragic events.

programme.title: The Best Version of Love
programme.desc: Image upgrader Rebecca discovers the best version of herself might include stubborn client Clive.

programme.title: Rule of Thirds

programme.title: Shaka Zulu
programme.desc: A female captive tries to dissuade the African leader (Henry Cele) from waging war against white settlers.

programme.title: Animal 2
programme.desc: James "Animal" Allen (Ving Rhames) took the fall for the crimes of his older son (Vicellous Shannon) and is now serving a life term at Folsom Prison. When his younger son, James Jr. (K.C. Collins), is framed for murder, Allen refuses to let two men from the same family submit to life imprisonment for crimes they didn't commit. When he learns that James Jr. was set up by someone at the prison, he resolves to win his son's freedom. To do this, he must return to his violent brawler roots.

programme.title: Mafia
programme.desc: In the 1970s, a fierce crime boss (Ving Rhames) wreaks havoc across the streets, and it's up to hardened detective James Womack (Pam Grier) and her partner Dupree (Robert Patrick) to put an end to organized crime - even if it means breaking the law.

programme.title: 10 Reasons Why Men Cheat
programme.desc: In the wake of a wedding catastrophe, three couples discover the meaning of commitment, while discussing what women want to know most of all when it comes to men and relationships. WHY DO MEN CHEAT?

programme.title: American Girl
programme.desc: After an argument with her emotionally abusive step-father, a smart 19-year-old woman from rural Georgia hastily moves to Atlanta with hopes of staying with a friend. Motivated by the social justice movement of 2020, she wants to work for Stacy Abrams and register people to vote. After an argument with her friend, she finds herself suddenly homeless and must survive on the mean streets of Atlanta by any means necessary.

programme.title: Father Knows Best
programme.desc: Zoe Miles, raised by a controlling father who introduces her to a life of crime, grapples with the lingering trauma of her mother's death. Yearning to embrace the spiritual life she shared with her grandmother, Zoe finds herself trapped in a cycle of violence, forced to rob drug dealers after witnessing a terrifying crime. Desperate to escape this destructive path, she struggles against her father's suffocating control, fearing the loss of his approval and the validation she desperately seeks.

programme.title: How Did We Get Here
programme.desc: Four struggling couples attend a wellness retreat led by Dr. Sellers. Melonie and Derrick struggle with issues of infidelity, Omar and Sonya struggle with commitment, Chastity and Monae have differing opinions on parenthood, and Stephanie and Delvin are struggling with their intimacy. As the couples dive into their treatment, they must confront their own demons to move forward. Will they make it out together? Or go their separate ways?

programme.title: Just Can't Leave You Alone
programme.desc: When a blind date ends in a shootout, Krystal Gray is left with more questions than answers. She discovers a flash drive linked to the murder and a web of secrets involving her brother, a dirty cop, and a mysterious man named Jarrell. As she uncovers the truth, Krystal becomes the target in a deadly game where love and betrayal blur and everyone has a hidden agenda.

programme.title: Hillside Cannibals
programme.desc: A true story that inspired THE HILLS HAVE EYES of a family who lived in caves and ate the flesh of passersby.

programme.desc: An authentic videotape reveals the shocking account of history's most infamous demonic possession.

programme.desc: "The Garden Party" - Mrs. Drysdale has a garden party and 'invites' the Clampetts by mistake. They end up back by their 'cement pond' to handle the 'overflow' from Mrs. Drysdale's party. A gun shot by Granny starts the real party.

programme.desc: "Turkey Day" - Elly bonds with the Thanksgiving turkey so the family can't bring themselves to kill it; Mrs Drysdale hires some Indians to take a Thanksgiving picture, which puts Granny on the warpath.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

Elly invites the filthy rich Fenwicks over, thinking they're destitute.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

Meanwhile, the Clampetts are looking forward to the Possum Day parade, which of course isn't celebrated in Beverly Hills, so Drysdale has to figure out how to have one to keep them happy.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: Metro
programme.desc: San Francisco hostage negotiator Scott Roper is partnered with rookie Kevin McCall. While Scott is trying to win back his girlfriend, Ronnie Tate, he and Kevin are called in to a jewel store, where psychotic Michael Korda has taken hostages. They manage to capture him -- but soon, Michael escapes from jail and kidnaps Ronnie as revenge. Despite officially being taken off the case, Scott and Kevin go after the dangerous criminal.

programme.title: Hard Rain
programme.desc: The small town of Huntingburg is forced to evacuate when torrential rains bring rising flood waters. The local sheriff (Randy Quaid) and town residents, having been through this before, are all too familiar with the looting and petty thefts that take place in an abandoned town. What they are not aware of, however, is the planned heist of an armored car sent to transport the $3 million from local banks.

programme.title: James May reassembles train
programme.desc: After the first episode of this series, you guys have gone nuts for this model train content! In part 1, Mike explained where the train came from (having been stuck in an attic for 50 years), and James May set about diagnosing the train's issues. So here is part 2, where things with Mike's model train restoration really start to take shape. It's time for more miniature engineering!

programme.title: Clarkson explains why cycling is actually bad for the environment
programme.desc: Jeremy has never been renowned for his love of cyclists and in our recent chat with him, he had plenty to say about them. Ferociously expensive cycle lanes, sweaty armpits and even their impact on the environment – it's fair to say he didn't hold back.

programme.title: Richard Hammond is having a mid-life crisis
programme.desc: Hammond is having a mid-life crisis, and he’s totally okay with it. He is embracing it with open arms, opting for the latest off-roader from Ford – the Ranger Raptor. With a meaty chassis and even meatier suspension, Richard can’t help taking the long way to work.

programme.title: Did James May just open the weirdest painting ever? | Mail Time Ep.4
programme.desc: It's episode four of James May's mail time and this time it's all about Clangers, train books and one of the strangest paintings we've ever seen. Be prepared to be disturbed.

programme.title: James May reviews his own cars – Tesla Model S vs Toyota Mirai
programme.desc: James May has bought two electric cars this year and they're at two very different ends of the EV scale. On one side, we have the incredibly quick Tesla Model S 100D, and on the other we have the Prius-based (and very comfortable) Toyota Mirai. Which one does he think will be the future? Let's find out!

programme.title: Scalextric car vs racing car drag race – which is quicker?
programme.desc: It's a question you've probably never asked – what is quicker, a racing car or a Scalextric car? We pitted little versus large (a real-life Jaguar I-Pace eTrophy against its slot car equivalent) to find out! We're backing Mike and the mini Jag.

programme.title: Richard Hammond reveals the new Land Rover Defender
programme.desc: After much anticipation, the new Land Rover Defender is finally here! Richard Hammond found a spanking new 90 and 110 in the middle of the woods somewhere and it looks like he approves of the redesign of the British off-roading icon. Watch as he takes you through all the main features of the 2020 Defender!

programme.title: James May explains how he got lost while filming Top Gear
programme.desc: It turns out that being filmed for Top Gear isn’t as easy as it sounds. Join James May down his local pub as he recalls the time he accidentally stalked an innocent family to their countryside home and had a bit of explaining to do. Doesn’t sound awkward at all.

programme.title: This is why racing cars are smaller than you think
programme.desc: You wouldn't say racing cars look particularly small, would you? Well, sit a prototype next to a GT racecar, and you'll suddenly realise just how tiny some racing cars are. Mike gets his models out to explain why some racing cars are much smaller than you think!

programme.title: Richard Hammond gave us a tour of his office!
programme.desc: You may or may not know that Richard Hammond has his own production company called Chimp. In this video, he takes us for a walk around the Chimp office while it's fairly deserted, giving us an insight into what he gets up to outside of The Grand Tour.

programme.title: James May roasts YouTubers' cars!
programme.desc: Once James May is on YouTube, no one is safe! Watch as James rates the most famous YouTuber cars – from Adam LZ's Zeroy to Jimmy Broadbent's R32 Skyline. Let the roast commence!

programme.title: James May can't take it anymore! | Mail Time FINALE!
programme.desc: After seven episodes, you guys finally cracked James May's will. The sheer number of packages you guys have sent to the DriveTribe office has blown our minds, but sadly this will be our final edition of Mail Time! Don't worry, we're going out with a cracker...

programme.title: The £2.5 million V12 GMA T.50 is the next McLaren F1 – and it uses a fan to create downforce
programme.desc: Gordon Murray blew the world away in the 1990s when he designed the McLaren F1, and now he's back with a brand new hypercar that uses a very similar recipe, apart from one vital ingredient. The GMA T.50 is a V12, manual, carbon fibre hypercar that uses a ground-effect fan to create more downforce than any road car before. Valkyrie, step aside, Professor Murray looks to have pulled a blinder with this £2.5 million weapon.

programme.title: Mike Brewer remembers Richard Hammond before he was on Top Gear | Q&A
programme.desc: With the latest season of Wheeler Dealers on the horizon, we grabbed Mike Brewer for a chat about the show and his love for cars, including the memories he has from working with The Grand Tour trio. He even remembers Hammond from his Renault PR days...

programme.title: This X20 engine is two V10s stuck together!
programme.desc: What happens when you take two V10 engines, gear them together and slap a Lamborghini gearbox on the other side? Well, you end up with an X20 engine, like this one built by the guys at Villiers for a Connaught supercar!

programme.title: This is how you develop a V12 from a 3-cylinder engine
programme.desc: It hopefully won't be long until we have two brand new V12 hypercars on the market – the Aston Martin Valkyrie and the Gordon Murray T.50 fan car. But the engines in these cars have more humble beginnings than you might think. Mike Fernie explains!

programme.title: The Bugatti Veyron was nearly powered by an even crazier engine than the W16
programme.desc: The Bugatti W16 engine has powered Veyrons and Chirons to some crazy top speeds, using its four turbochargers and many, many radiators. But did you know that the company nearly opted for an even crazier engine? One that makes the W16 seem small?! Mike Fernie explains!

programme.title: I drove Jeremy Clarkson's Alfa Romeo and he wasn't happy
programme.desc: We recently headed up to Chipping Norton to film with Jeremy at his farm, so we thought it would be a great chance to get up close and personal with his beloved Alfa Romeo. We received permission from Jeremy's partner, but after taking to the wheel, Mike quickly found out that we really needed to run it past Clarkson himself. Here's what happened...

programme.title: James May gave us a tour of his new pub
programme.desc: You may have heard in recent weeks that James May has bought half a pub. Although it's still having finishing touches added to it ahead of its opening next week, James kindly offered to give us a sneak peek at what's to come.

programme.title: James May's first look at the 2000hp Lotus Evija
programme.desc:
James May loves looking into the future of the automotive landscape, and he did just that through a day with the stunning Lotus Evija hypercar.
2000hp, £2 million and all the EV geekery you could possibly hope for. Take it away, Mr May...

programme.title: We reunited Richard Hammond with his old Ferrari 550 | ft @harrysgarage
programme.desc: Mike Fernie has travelled across the country to fetch a pretty special car from Richard Hammond's past – a 1998 Ferrari Maranello that was also once owned by YouTube legend Harry Metcalfe. Be prepared, this video is FULL of nostalgia!

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Jaguar restoration hasn't gone to plan
programme.desc: 2020 was the year of the Jaguar for Richard Hammond, with the DriveTribe founder kicking off the restoration of two classic Jags – and XK120 and an XK150. The '150 is very nearly finished, but a mechanical gremlin has stopped this beauty from hitting the road.

programme.title: Was Clarkson right about the MG SV having a 1000bhp Nitrous Oxide kit?
programme.desc: Do you remember MG's supercar from 2004, called the MG SV? Well Mike Fernie has managed to get his hands on one, and looks to dispel the myth that was once uttered on Top Gear via Jeremy Clarkson. Did this brute of a car actually come with a 1000bhp Nitrous Oxide kit? Let's find out...

programme.title: This is why James May is selling his Ferrari...
programme.desc: Yup, you've read that correctly, James has put his Ferrari up for sale. Listen as he explains why he has decided to let it go...

programme.title: This car shows why V6s are awesome
programme.desc:
Everyone seemed to hate the V6 Mustang, but Ginetta has taken that 3.7-litre Ford V6 and worked some serious magic with it in the G55 GT4 racing car. Mike Fernie takes to the Grand Prix circuit at Silverstone to see what's what.
The car Mike is driving is specifically the Ginetta GT Academy car, which is priced at £62,500; that's pretty excellent value for a track day car! And if you have £119,000 you could find yourself in the full GT4 car, ready for a trip to the Nürburgring 24 Hours...

programme.title: Meet the YouTuber who lives in a shed but owns an R32 Skyline GT-R
programme.desc: Mike took a trip down to the south coast of England to catch up with Jimmy Broadbent, a sim racing YouTuber who has built an incredibly successful channel – from a shed at the bottom of his mum's garden. But it turns out Jimmy very much has his priorities straight, seeing as he owns a mint Nissan Skyline R32 GT-R. That's about as good as man maths can possibly get.

programme.title: Was James May right about classic cars being rubbish?
programme.desc: A few videos ago, James May said that he was 'over' classic car ownership and is now moving on into the futuristic tech of batteries and hydrogen. This has baffled many of us, so much so that Mike Fernie has decided to grab the keys to arguably the greatest classic car of all time – the Series 1 Jaguar E-Type – to see whether what May has said is true.

programme.title: Porsche has made a Formula 1 engine that could power a new hypercar
programme.desc: After the incredibly successful Porsche 919 LMP1 programme, the engineering team decided to develop their technology further, with the aim being to enter F1 as an engine supplier. That wasn't to be, but there could be another use for the engine that was developed...

programme.title: How is Richard Hammond's classic car business doing since his TV show went live?
programme.desc: With 2023 coming to a close, Mike Fernie grabs Chief Cog Neil Greenhouse for a walkaround of Richard Hammond's workshop to discuss the cars inside and how the business is doing after its second year in business.

programme.title: Is this electric MG the PERFECT car for James May?
programme.desc: James May has been outspoken about how he wants to move on from classic car motoring, instead vouching for electric or hydrogen modes of transport. Mike Fernie has decided to provide a happy medium, to see if he can convince May to at least have some classic motoring in his life. Enter the RBW EV Roadster!

programme.title: Why are almost all modern engines 2.0-litres?
programme.desc: There is one engine that is becoming dominant in the car world – the 2.0-litre four-cylinder. V12s, V10s, V8s and V6s are all on the way out, making way for these turbo fours. But why is it now the engine layout and size of choice? Mike Fernie digs deep into the engineering of the modern day engine to find out.

programme.title: Is Richard Hammond taking Oliver back to Botswana?!
programme.desc: Oliver is arguably one of the most beloved car characters from the trio's history, and Hammond famously brought the Opel Kadett back to the UK to join his car collection. In this behind the scenes look at Hammond's commute to his new business, The Smallest Cog, Richard hints that he may end up making the trip back to Africa one day...

programme.title: A day in the life of Richard Hammond!

programme.title: I've Been Killing Slimes For 300 Years And Maxed Out My Level
programme.desc: Azusa receives an invitation from Beelzebub to attend a ceremony in the land of the demons. It seems she earned a favorable reputation with the demons after stopping the conflict between the two Dragon clans in Laika's homeland. Azusa invites the whole family along for this high honor, but there's one problem: Rosalie, the ghost, can't wear a dress. But Rosalie is part of the family, and Azusa wants to help her dress up like the rest of them, so she uses the power of her maxed level to create a magic spell...

programme.title: I've Been Killing Slimes For 300 Years And Maxed Out My Level
programme.desc: Halkara's knack for inadvertently causing trouble led her to knock out the Demon King Pecora, and now she's locked in a dungeon. As Halkara's execution draws nearer with each passing moment, Azusa rushes to make a potion with her family's help and, with Beelzebub's guidance, takes it to the first-aid room where Pecora is. After tricking the demons guarding the room, Azusa finally reaches Pecora, but then...

programme.title: I've Been Killing Slimes For 300 Years And Maxed Out My Level
programme.desc: Laika and Flatorte, the newest member of the family, are constantly bickering. Their tribes do have a history of conflict, but it seems the fact that they haven't been eating as much meat lately is contributing to their irritability. Just then, Natalie from the guild arrives and tells them about a quest to get rid of a massive number of longhammer boars, the perfect quest to fill their hungry bellies...

programme.title: I've Been Killing Slimes For 300 Years And Maxed Out My Level
programme.desc: Falfa woke up in the form of a slime! Of course, that's not too shocking since she's a slime spirit, but her sister Shalsha laments that this has never happened before and she doesn't know how to change her back.So Azusa sets off with Shalsha and Beelzebub in search of an "unusual slime" who might know a way to change Falfa back...

programme.title: I've Been Killing Slimes For 300 Years And Maxed Out My Level
programme.desc: One day, a girl with rabbit ears comes to the village of Flatta. This nameless, unpopular minstrel, who strums her lute fiercely with her bare fingers and sings with a death growl, is known as Schifanoia. When she passes out in the middle of her concert, Azusa is reminded of the sad stories she's heard of young men who moved to Tokyo hoping to make their major debut, but instead turn into job-hopping middle-aged men playing in bands that never make any profit. So she invites Schifanoia to live at the house in the highlands for a while, but when she just keeps making excuses and refuses to see reality, Flatorte loses her patience...

programme.title: I've Been Killing Slimes For 300 Years And Maxed Out My Level
programme.desc: Halkara throws a mushroom-grilling party, or "shroompa" for short, and accidently includes some poisonous mushrooms that cause anyone who eats them to shrink... and Azusa eats one. This leads to everyone ridiculing her,or rather, fawning over how cute she looks. Even her daughters are in on it! Azusa is determined to change back to normal, so after a tip from Pecora, she heads to the World Tree along with Fatla and Vania to find a pharmacist who deals in every kind of medicine. She expects a dungeon with 108 floors to fight through, but...

programme.title: I've Been Killing Slimes For 300 Years And Maxed Out My Level
programme.desc: Festival season has come to the village of Flatta. The festival has been held there for the past 250 years, but as the only person who knows its entire history since it was started, Azusa has distanced herself from it to let the villagers keep their independence. But her family has shown interest in it and seems excited to see it, so she makes up her mind to change the way she interacts with the village tradition... and thus the one-day-only coffee shop, The Witch's House, was born. Will the villagers enjoy this little shop Azusa's family has created?

programme.title: Miss Kobayashi's Dragon Maid
programme.desc: One morning, as Miss Kobayashi left for work, she found a dragon before her. The dragon transformed into a beautiful girl with horns, a tail, and a maid outfit!

programme.title: The Eminence in Shadow
programme.desc: Alpha faces off against an empowered Nelson in a vicious battle. Elsewhere, Cid and Aurora reach the Sanctuary's core, but a door only a hero can open blocks their path.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League

Winner of several SCL stages in the past.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Four times Strongest Man of Holland, Alex Moonen and the “Flying Dutchman” Kelvin de Ruiter competing here in Holland against 12 other strongest men in the world. programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Strongest Man of Holland, Alex Moonen and the “Flying Dutchman” Kelvin de Ruiter, competing in Holland against 12 other strongest men in the world. The fifth event, a front hold of 25 kg is loved or hated by the athletes and lastly, the Stones of Strength.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: An unbelievable TANK PULL of 50.000 kg was at the stage of SCL RUSSIA. Never seen this before! With Mikhail Koklyaev also as event presenter, the former top 3 world strongest man from Russia was there

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave cooks jangjorim, a Korean braised dish usually served as a side, but that Dave thinks could be an entree and a star. He also discusses how to know when rice is properly washed, as well as the difference between mu radish and daikon (amongst other Korean and Japanese foods). Dave finishes the episode by answering an Ask Dave about who he would want as company for his dream last meal and his answer is...surprising.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave is joined by a friend of the podcast, Noelle, partner at Majordomo Media, as he cooks her a meaningful dish that she did not know he was going to make. They talk about Paris (related to the dish), changes in the global dining scene, and some necessary improvements on the rules of sharing food.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave talks to Ruby Tandoh, bestselling food writer and author of 'All-Consuming: Why We Eat the Way We Eat Now,' about the big topics in food media today. From what food criticism looks like with the new democratization of influencer-critics to the future of recipe creation, Dave and Ruby discuss complicated questions that many in food culture are asking. Dave answers an Ask Dave, then finishes with the first of a two-part ramen recipe by making the noodles.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave is in New York at Infatuation's food festival, EEEEEASTCON NYC, with special guests: chef Justin Pichetrungsi of Anajak Thai and author, director, and chef Eddie Huang. The show starts with a New York City food Overrated/Underrated, and then the trio gives out their 'Best of EEEEEATSCON' Awards: Best Fried, Best Sandwich, and Best Sweet. They finish with their Best in Show award.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Chicken-Bacon-Ranch “Larb”: Andrew prepares a dish from Dinner Time Live that is a testament to ranch dressing. More specifically, a testament to the holy trinity of chicken, bacon, and ranch. This riff on larb is full of flavor and meat. "Riff” because larb is the national dish of Laos — as well as a very popular dish in northern Thailand — and this is by no means an authentic larb. But… is it delicious? Yeah, it is.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Pan Roasted Salmon with Caper Butter Sauce, Green Beans & Potato Rösti - Andrew prepares another delicious meal from the Classic Comforts menu of Dinner Time Live: Pan roasted, dry aged salmon with a caper butter sauce, and a buttery potato rösti, basically a fancy-sounding Swiss-German term for hash browns.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipe - XO Fries & Crispy Shrimp Rolls - Chris and Andrew make two more recipes from Dinner Time Live with David Chang: XO Fries and Crispy Shrimp Rolls. The XO Fries start with a bed of golden French Fries, then topped with a pile of Chinese BBQ pork and covered with a savory, slightly spicy XO sauce (made from dried seafood, ham, shallots and chili oil). The Crispy Shrimp Rolls are inspired by the famous crispy shrimp tacos from Mariscos Jalisco—a legendary food truck in LA.

programme.title: Behind the Counter
programme.desc: Behind the Counter: Dave Chang makes Silence of the Lambs and Scream inspired dishes, and behind the scenes at Majordomo - In this episode, we give you a tour of Majordomo Studios and a behind the scenes look at Chef David Chang's creative process as he and his team come up with ideas for the Halloween episode of Dinner Time Live, a weekly live cooking show on Netflix. Dave riffs on concepts from Silence of the Lambs and Scream. The Majordomo team celebrates Halloween. Andrew makes family meal.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter with David Chang - A Little Bit of Everything: An unfiltered behind-the-scenes look into David Chang's kitchen as he and his team develop and practice a unique menu in the days leading up to the next Netflix episode of Dinner Time Live. This episode sees Dave working on "A Little Bit of Everything" menu for comedian Jay Pharoah and rapper Lil Jon. Dishes include: Tempura Rock Shimp with Salsa Seca, Everything Bagel-Spice Flatbread, and Boiled Chicken Two Ways.

programme.title: Great Chefs of America
programme.desc: Recipes included with this episode: Crispy Mirliton and Spicy Shrimp Napoleon, Bourride with Aioli, and Lemon-Goat Cheese Cheesecake. Restaurants include: Cuvée in New Orleans, Café des Artistes in New York, and NOMI in Chicago.

programme.title: Great Chefs of America
programme.desc: Recipes included with this episode: Crab Cocktail, Potato Chip Sandwich with Scallops, Caviar, and Bass Tartare, and Oven-roasted Kobocha Squash Masala Dosa. Restaurants include: Judson Grill in New York, Patina in Los Angeles, and Zealous in Chicago.

programme.title: Great Chefs of America
programme.desc: Recipes included with this episode: Foie Gras, Apple, and Goat Cheese Creme Brulee, Chicken Breast with Fennel, Peaches, and Roses, and Pear Tart Tatin with MapleWalnut and Sour Cherry Compote. Restaurants include: Cuvée in New Orleans, L'Etoile in Madison, and Judson Grill in New York.

programme.title: Great Chefs of America
programme.desc: Recipes included with this episode: Frog Legs Spiced with Pickle Masala, Grilled Portobello, and Chocolate-Passion Fruit. Restaurants include: Guastavino's in New York, The Sheraton in Washington, and NOMI in Chicago.

programme.title: Great Chefs of America
programme.desc: Recipes included with this episode: Shirred Eggs with White Truffles, Oven-roasted Chicken with Soft Mascarpone Polenta, and Three-Milk Cake. Restaurants include: March in New York, Caliterra in Chicago, and Maurice French Pastry in Metairie.

programme.title: Great Chefs of America
programme.desc: Recipes included with this episode: Duck Confit with Crepe and Berry Compote, Cote de Boeuf with Market Vegetables, and Warm Pear and Chocolate-Cinnamon Tart. Restaurants include: Michabelle in Hammond, Club Guastavino's in New York, and The American Restaurant in Kansas City.

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home
programme.desc: “There’s nothing as good as a good piece of meat”, and as Julia is quick to point out, there is less and less really good (well marbled and aged) beef to be found. Hangar Steak, Skirt Strip, Flank and Ribeye.

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques and Julia create some very special desserts in this colorful episode. Poires Belle Helene - poached pear perched atop pound cake drizzled with chocolate sauce; sweet creamy rich and delicious Strawberries Sabayon; and Crepes Suzette, classically flambéed and served with orange butter and powdered sugar, as well as a Hungarian inspired torta of layered crepes baked with raspberry jam.

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home - Salads
programme.desc: Jacques dressed in a toga? For the Julia's Caesar Salad, of course! This show has a salad for everyone, from a simple mixed green salad with a garlic vinaigrette, to classic Caesar and Nicoise (with fresh seared tuna) Salads. How about some Potato Salad? Well, Julia and Jacques prepare two: an American style potato salad with home made mayonnaise, and its tart French cousin.

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home - Sandwiches
programme.desc: As a child, one of Julia’s favorite sandwiches was an ice cream sandwich. Jacques was a stick of chocolate between two pieces of bread. Today, Julia’s favorite is the Croque Monsieur, or pressed and baked ham & cheese. Of course, Jacques provides a variation he calls a Croque Madam. A close second they can both agree on is the New England lobster roll, served on a toasted hot dog bun.

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home - Vegetables
programme.desc: Julia and Jacques prepare artichokes—several ways—including artichoke hearts stuffed with spinach and mushroom Duxelle. Julia demonstrates her secret to ever-green cooked string beans and butter bruised peas. Jacques counters with his aromatic Tomato Provencal.

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home - Beef Stew
programme.desc: Sounds rather ordinary for this celebrated duet…but here, Jacques and Julia demonstrate why Beef Burgundy and Pot Roast have beens favorite “classic” recipes (here and in France) for what seems like “forever,” and they put their own signature on it “Swiss cheese mashed potatoes” (for the Beef Burgundy), and “soft ribbon noodles” (fort the Pot Roast).

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home - Fish
programme.desc: Sole food and more. One of Julia’s most memorable meals was the Sole Meuniere she had on first arriving in Paris after WWII. With Jacques, she recreates this dish, as delightful as ever. The sole is followed by grilled halibut with herbed butter, and poached snapper with beautifully turned and sautéed cucumbers.

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home - Turkey
programme.desc: This meal is the holiday favorite that is so good, Julia & Jacques recommend it for any day of the year. Roast Turkey with sausage and cornbread stuffing, gravy made the old-fashioned way, accompanied by creamed onions and a zesty Cranberry-Apple Chutney. The “not-quite” which has to be seen, is the inventive way the turkey is cut-up before cooking, roasted and re-assembled for serving.

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home - Soup
programme.desc: Four classic soups made from scratch in Julia’s kitchen: aromatic steaming hot French Onion Soup; Vichyssoise, or chilled Leek and Potato soup, as well as an original variation on the theme using watercress; and a hearty Mediterranean fish stew with an authentic rouille. And, because no show about would be complete without Chicken soup, we are presented chicken & noodle and chicken & rice soups.

programme.title: Julia & Jacques Cooking at Home - Eggs
programme.desc: Eggs: all sizes, all types from chicken to quail to ostrich… soft, scrambled, poached… from omelets to Frittatas to eggs Benedict…not to mention Julia and Jacques scrambling an ostrich egg as voluminous as a dozen and a half Grade A Large.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Learning about Greystone: With Mar's own home's projects finally complete, it's time for him to get on to the newest big-scale project. Paul has cooked up a surprise: a wonderful new renovation project at a home called Greystone. It needs to be completely overhauled so its true potential can shine. But Paul hasn't told Mar everything and there is quite an unexpected discovery awaiting him.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The Renovation continues: Despite the extent of the work needed, the walls start to come down and a brand new, state-of-the-art home begins to rise in its place. There are a lot of moving parts to keep track of. The first completely finished rooms? 4 completely renovated bathrooms. Taking all 4 from down-to-the-studs to spa-like havens is no easy task but the results are Marvellous.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The big renovation of Greystone is at the turning point: time for Mar to turn his attention to creating or updating 4 bedrooms-each with their own en suite! No detail is too small to make it happen: move windows, closets, hallways-heck, move everything! It's a down-to-the-studs approach that he hopes will really pay off. Plus: Mar MC’s a charity dance competition & takes the stage with celebs.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: This 4-part home makeover is ready for its grand finale (and a party!) All the areas that the new owners and their guests will use and see every day are done-so the stakes are high! All the modern technological touches call for a special reveal. So Mar throws a party-and has his own surprises waiting for his guests, too. It's Casual Luxury come to life!

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: 4 seasons. 4 apartments. 1 building full of design. Watch these Margical makeovers of 4 different apartments. Mar gets challenged to bring 4 new spaces to life-but without any renovation! Watch him take the 4 seasons as inspiration for the 4 apartments. And thanks to help from Ethan Allen these new apartments inspire everyone who visits. Will your favorite be Fall, Winter, Spring or Summer?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Oops! Can't believe we did that. Yikes! We're going to show you anyway. New! Behind the scenes footage you've never seen. Regular episodes get full fast. (And it's not always smooth sailing.) This fan-favorite episode is stuffed with things you've never seen. Behind-the-scenes & reveals; slip-ups & bloopers & outright embarrassing mistakes. Casual Luxury never looked so, well, casual!

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Think small! Small spaces are the new big trend in design. Mar is the master of small space transformation - and shows how it can be applied by staging 2 very different spaces: a small cape style home and a newly constructed apartment complex, both in downtown Westport, CT. You won’t believe how spacious these small footprint Casual Luxury reveals are.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The Cornwall Castle, Part 1: Mar gets a phone call from another time period. The new owners of an honest-to-goodness castle want to restore and renovate their new property using Mar's 6 Casual Luxury design principles as a guide. It could be a Margical makeover of this hidden-away treasure, but there is a lot of work to do. Mar meets the enthusiastic family and learns their era-honoring plans.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The Cornwall Castle, Part 2: Mar lets down the drawbridge to show you the big reveal! Mar heads back to the castle to check the progress, and for the grand tour of the final results. His 6 Casual Luxury design principles were the owners' guide, and they definitely work in every time period! Watch this distressed property find it's "happily ever after"! (It's a Wow!)

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: We're celebrating 40! 40 original episodes, that is. So Mar’s looking back at three full seasons of Casual Luxury designs. Our favorite moments and yours. Join Mar and the Team on this very special episode as we look back at many of the places, characters, fun, makeovers and trips that make the series special—plus a rare look at the very beginning and some new reveals, too. Celebrate with us!

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Meet the Breslin family, new to Fairfield CT, and their fixer-upper back yard and guest cottage. Mar thinks he's all set after they move in to the main house, but when he agrees to work on their outdoor living spaces he has no idea what he's in for. But the transformation leaves them amazed. Plus: Mar does a little free-falling without a parachute.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: It might be Fall in New England, but Mar makes it easy to get in the swim of things! This incredible backyard makeover & reveal will make you (and the makeover family) smile from ear to ear. A year in the making, its a transformation you won't want to miss. But after Mar plays a reporter in a shark attack movie will you be afraid to go back in the water? Watch him film it and find out!

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Will Mar's design dreams go up in smoke? With some spare time between projects, Mar turns his attention to his own home, Rosebrook Gardens. This time: updating and reworking an entire side of the house: fireplace, mantel, hearth & chimney. Plus new patio, landscaping, outdoor furnishings & accessories. In a twist, Mar hands off the final reveal to Team Mar, will it be up to standards?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: It’s definitely fireplace weather in New England, and Mar has got that covered—outdoors, too! While Mar’s colleague Paul is away on business, Mar goes into overdrive to transform Rosebrook Gardens. And local resources pull together to make it happen. New windows and doors, terrace, fire pit, furniture, and landscaping. It’s a transformation no one could have expected, especially Paul.

Taking all 4 from down-to-the-studs to spa-like havens is no easy task but the results are Marvellous.

programme.desc: Witness the drive and ambition behind Haarlem Meets Harlem, meet a variety of business executives and entrepreneurs that are taking Harlem, and the world, by storm, and learn how Chef Marcus Samuelsson has become an inspiration to many.

programme.title: Laura McKenzie's Traveler
programme.desc: Rent a Rolls Royce and roll through L.A. in style. When you hit Hollywood and Vine you know you are in the city's heart, as the Pantages Theater, Capitol Records Building, Grauman's Chinese Theatre, and Kodak Theater greet you. Get the skinny on everything Beverly Hills and visit Olvera Street.

programme.title: Laura McKenzie's Traveler
programme.desc: A delightful Mediterranean climate, fantastic beaches, and fascinating historic sites only begin to describe the Maltese experience. Few places in the world possess a more exquisite coastline. The HBO series, "Game of Thrones," filmed portions of the series in Malta's capital city of Valletta.

programme.title: Laura McKenzie's Traveler
programme.desc: Athens is an exceptional country to experience history. First, visit Syntagma Square and Greece's famous landmark- The Acropolis. An excursion to Greece is nothing without a trip to the Agora, as we finish off by enjoying Athens' most abundant resource- water.

programme.title: Laura McKenzie's Traveler
programme.desc: The first sight is Singapore's Merlion statue. For glimpses of the wild you have three fun options where you can see exotic animals in their open (not closed) sanctuaries. Next, explore Raffles Landing for a picture-perfect view along the Singapore River.

programme.title: Laura McKenzie's Traveler
programme.desc: There is more than enough to keep you busy in Paris. Historic sites such as the Louvre, Notre Dame Cathedral, the Picasso Museum, and Paris Opera House only get you started. Tuileries Garden and Paris Beach offer a unique way to relax and have fun, and Sacre-Coeur offers a 360-degree view of city.

programme.title: Laura McKenzie's Traveler
programme.desc: At the confluence of Muir Eireann and the Irish Sea on Ireland's East Coast stands the capital city of Dublin. Join Laura McKenzie as she enjoys the perfect pint at Gravity Bar atop Guinness Brewery, explore Dublin Castle, and become acquainted with The Four Seasons Dublin.

programme.title: Family Travel with Colleen Kelly
programme.desc: Colleen learns the significance of the traditional Stomp Dance and the history of the Chickasaw People, tries her hand at a game of Stickball, and tastes the local cuisine. One of the most delicious treats? Fried pies at Arbuckle Mountain.

programme.title: Travels with Darley
programme.desc: Your revolutionary road trip in South Carolina with Darley continues. From Charleston to Camden, the state's two oldest cities, you will learn much more about the founding of America, and its true potential, through the lens of history.

programme.title: Travels with Darley
programme.desc: Travel to the heart of Cajun Country as Darley attends the annual Festival International de Louisiane in Lafayette, Louisiana. You will also experience crawfishing, Cajun cooking, Zydeco dancing, and tasty cuisine. Let the good times roll.

programme.title: Tastes Like Home
programme.desc: His love of horses provided Peter with the impetus to live on Jeju Island, South Korea. With a desire to send Peter a taste from home, his wife Grainne teaches Catherine how to make his favorite dish of roast stuffed fillet of pork.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a giant church complex, the DomQuartier, in this tiny Alpine town, and why is there an equally giant medieval fortress high above it? Who lived in Mirabell Palace, and Hellbrun Palace? Why is Getreidegassse so charming? And how do you solve a problem like Maria?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why does Paris look like it does? Why were the Eiffel Tower and the Pont Alexandre III built? Why is the Opera Garnier so ornate? Why is there an eastern-style basilica atop the highest point in Paris? Why was a cabaret built inside a red mill (the Moulin Rouge)?

programme.title: What's Eating Harlem
programme.desc: Meet the mastermind behind Lady Lexis Sweets and the delicious treats found there, visit Ginny's Supper Club for an intimate conversation with Author Monica Rich Kosann and Journalist Tamron Hall, and experience the energy of Double Dutch.

programme.title: What's Eating Harlem
programme.desc: Find out how to make the perfect Cuban sandwich at Pilar Cuban Eatery in Brooklyn, meet the fabulous people behind the film Chapter & Verse at the Cove Lounge in Harlem, and celebrate Charles Rangel, a man with deep roots in Harlem.

programme.title: Andiamo! Uptown
programme.desc: Donna explores Dubai, one of the gems of the Middle East. While there, she discovers Emirati culture and cuisine, experiences an upscale desert safari, goes scuba diving, and more. There is so much to experience in Dubai.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Italy is one of the very few nations in the world that regularly gets three major extreme natural phenomena – volcanos, flooding, and avalanches. Eastern flanks of the volcano to slip by up to two metres, and many houses on the flanks of the volcano experienced structural damage. The eruption also completely destroyed the Rifugio Sapienza, on the southern flank of the volcano. The Rifugio was the site of a cable car station which had previously been destroyed in the 1983 eruption; both have now been rebuilt. The most recent eruption was in November 2006.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: It is time for “Angry Planet” to do a serious desert episode. What better place to do it than this fabled city in the middle of the mighty Sahara Desert.This almost mythical place really does exist. And it really is still possible to travel there by camel caravan, riding the infamous “Ships of the Desert”. Extreme heat, dehydration, possible sand storms, frigid night temperatures and desert predators are our potential companions for the trip.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Geoorge explores Java at the heart of the Ring of Fire.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Possibly the home of the deadly Marberg Ebola virus, Kitum Cave is high on Mt.Elgon straddling the Kenya-Uganda border. The deep, waterfall-shrouded cave has been carved out over millennia by elephants in search of salt. When host george kourounis gets bitten by one of the bats…well, you’ll have to see the show to find out what happens.

programme.title: Angry Planet
